text:
REGIME — Broad risk-on bounce, tech and industrials led, energy sold hard.

INDICES — VIX 24.5, falling. QQQ +1.24%, SPY +0.75%, IWM +0.63%, DIA +0.49%. Large caps outpaced small caps today. Gold surged +1.75%, the standout cross-asset move, while USO dropped -2.48% and TLT fell -0.50%.

SECTORS — XLI +1.67%, XLK +1.51%, XLB +0.98%, XLV +0.76%, XLY +0.75%, XLU +0.48%, XLC +0.34%, XLRE +0.29%, XLF +0.14%, XLP -0.63%, XLE -3.74%. Top industries: GDX +4.62% and JETS +2.60%. Bottom: FCG -3.34% and XOP -3.84%.

THE 7s — Fab 7 +4.12%, Old Metals 7 +3.90%, Semi 7 +3.13%, Defense 7 +2.52%, Buildout 7 +2.27%, Industrial 7 +2.16%, New Metals 7 +1.77%, Bank 7 +0.94%, Health 7 +0.94%, Housing 7 +0.78%, Freight 7 +0.64%, Cloud 7 +0.59%, Power 7 +0.51%, New Staples 7 +0.36%, Medtech 7 +0.01%, Insurance 7 -0.26%, Software 7 -0.46%, Toll Road 7 -0.51%, Old Staples 7 -0.84%, Energy 7 -3.59%. Strongest ticker in Fab 7 drove the basket; weakest in Energy 7 dragged that group to session lows.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed neutral at 50.1. Top movers: GDX +4.62% (sectors/industries), Fab 7 +4.12% (baskets), GREK +3.33% (vol spike). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +5.63%; worst: XLP -6.01%. Best basket: Energy 7 +8.52%; worst: Housing 7 -9.85%. Brent at $121.88 pressured energy equities despite elevated crude prices, signaling margin-compression fears over raw demand strength.

WATCH — GDX, +4.62%, vol spike on gold momentum diverging from 20d -7.20% mean reversion setup worth monitoring. RSPU, +0.55%, 10.2x volume spike with muted price suggests positioning rotation deserves scrutiny. Software 7 constituent via FNGS +2.05% at 2.4x vol while Software 7 sits -25.18% YTD — watch for dead-cat bounce or genuine inflection.
